name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) will take place in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials, and focuses on the themes of Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism for PhD students, young researchers, and senior scholars. The program also includes networking opportunities, social events such as a reception at the Vienna City Hall, and participation from leading experts across AI sub‑disciplines.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
venue: Skeikampen Conference Hotel, near Oslo, Norway
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is organized by the Nordic Language Processing Laboratory and the University of Oslo in Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries and doctoral students, and focuses on multilingual large language models, their evaluation, and data curation. The event is held at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo and includes technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and a bus transfer from Oslo Airport.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The Probabilistic AI School 2026 will take place in Vilnius, Lithuania from August 3‑7, 2026. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment covering state‑of‑the‑art machine learning and artificial intelligence topics. Enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals are invited to participate.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
venue: Durham University, Durham, England, UK
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England, UK. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a broad spectrum of computer‑vision topics, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The program is designed for graduate students and early‑career researchers seeking hands‑on experience and exposure to current advances in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: University of Oxford, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 is a specialised AI school hosted by the University of Oxford with an online component. It offers three specialised tracks taught by world‑renowned experts from frontier AI labs in academia and industry, allowing participants to customise their learning by selecting any combination of tracks. The programme aims to upskill attendees in the latest AI theory and applications through a unique, flexible experience.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It brings together doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to discuss topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making through keynotes, industry talks, brainstorming sessions and student presentations. The program is hosted at the Happy Tammsvik resort and includes indoor and outdoor ice‑breaker activities, with all accommodation and meals sponsored for participants.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
venue: Università di Genova, Genova, Italy
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held in Edinburgh, United Kingdom, from 22 to 26 June 2026. It offers expert lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art robotic technology. The school targets master’s‑level students and industry practitioners, providing networking opportunities with academics and industry leaders.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
venue: Hotel Dei Pini, Porto Conte, near Alghero, Sardinia, Italy
description: The 2026 International Summer School on Biometrics takes place at Hotel Dei Pini in Porto Conte, near Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, from 8 to 12 June 2026. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers, forensic examiners and professionals, offering lectures by 20 leading experts, hands‑on sessions, keynote talks, and opportunities for scholarships and paper presentations.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
venue: Porto – Maia, Portugal
description: AIces 2026 is the first International School on the Cognitive, Ethical and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence, held in Porto, Portugal from March 30 to April 2, 2026. The program targets graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering parallel courses, a symposium, and open debates covering cognition, ethics, and society in AI. Participants can attend face‑to‑face sessions, join live online, and receive recordings for 45 days after the event, with a certificate of participation.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
venue: Umeå University, Umeå, Sweden
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems is held at Umeå University in Sweden. It offers lectures, multidisciplinary hands‑on training, a poster session, and a social program to foster networking among students, researchers, and professionals. The three‑day event runs from 11 to 13 March 2026 and focuses on the societal implications and responsible development of AI and autonomous technologies.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
venue: Escandille, Autrans, France
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM) 2026 will be held at the Escandille in Autrans, France. It aims to bring together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech/audio processing, and human‑robot interaction to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange through courses, talks, and informal networking. The program features expert lectures on topics like affective signal processing, conversational speech synthesis, and multimodal dialogue, as well as a hands‑on session on human‑robot interaction.

name: Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) Melbourne
venue: Sandridge Events Centre, Port Melbourne, Australia
description: The MLSS 2026 Summer School in Melbourne brings together 50 hand‑picked PhD students and early‑career researchers for two weeks of intensive machine‑learning training. World‑class speakers from academia and industry cover topics ranging from core theory to emerging research beyond large language models, including deep learning, reinforcement learning, and geometric deep learning. The in‑person event is held at the Sandridge Events Centre in Port Melbourne, offering modern facilities and a vibrant setting for lectures, tutorials, and networking.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Winter School on Deep Learning is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference in Tromsø, Norway. It runs from 11 to 15 January 2027 and focuses on state‑of‑the‑art deep‑learning techniques such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time‑series prediction and speech recognition. The school brings together national and international researchers to exchange ideas, foster collaborations, and present cutting‑edge research.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
venue: Swostishree Gurukul, Sanobharyang, Kathmandu, Nepal
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work. It targets senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals, bringing together over 500 in‑person attendees and thousands of livestream participants. The school aims to provide fundamental AI training, inspire early‑stage researchers from Nepal and the region, and foster collaboration among leading scientists worldwide.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School, Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. It offers a series of lectures and tutorials on machine‑learning research presented by leading experts from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, EPFL and the University of Oxford. The program is aimed at graduate students and early‑career researchers interested in advanced topics and networking within the Eastern European AI community.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program that brings together early‑stage researchers, practitioners and AI experts for keynotes, lectures and hands‑on sessions. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and professionals, focusing on topics such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models, reinforcement learning and ethics in machine learning. The school also provides networking opportunities through poster sessions, social activities and informal interactions, and will be held in Spain in September 2027.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI takes place at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco. It offers a five‑day immersive program for master, Ph.D., post‑doc and early‑career researchers, featuring lectures, hands‑on labs, project work and networking with leading AI experts. Organized jointly by MoroccoAI, INPT, IMT Nord Europe and Soft Centre and supported by ACM SIGMM, the school provides cutting‑edge knowledge on multimodal models and generative AI.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
venue: NCSR Demokritos, Athens, Greece
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 will be held at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. The program, organized by RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business and Heriot‑Watt University, focuses on advanced topics in natural language processing. It runs from 2 to 8 September 2026 and is linked to the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in September.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school at Lady Margaret Hall focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It offers theoretical foundations and hands‑on experience with clustering, GANs, self‑supervised, and energy‑based models across various real‑world domains. The programme is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML knowledge who are considering graduate study or research.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI brings together researchers, PhD students and post‑doctoral scholars in Caen, Normandy, France. It focuses on applying deep learning techniques to accelerate scientific research, covering topics such as machine‑learning for data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and integration of AI with physical simulations. The program combines lectures with hands‑on workshops using Python libraries like NumPy and PyTorch, fostering interdisciplinary collaboration and practical skill development.

name: LUT Summer School
venue: LUT University, Lappeenranta, Finland
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive programme held at the Lappeenranta campus offering courses on sustainability, climate action, marketing, management, and the intersection of business and technology. It is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students from any university who wish to gain 3 ECTS credits while experiencing Finnish summer nature and a vibrant social programme. The school combines classroom teaching, workshops, group projects and extracurricular activities such as sauna evenings and outdoor excursions.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
venue: Università di Genova, Genoa, Italy
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School, a Machine Learning Crash Course, takes place at the University of Genova in Genoa, Italy. It offers an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms through lectures and hands‑on lab sessions, targeting undergraduate and graduate students as well as professionals. The program is held entirely in person, includes daily lunches, and provides university credits upon successful completion of the final test.
